basic_details.job_title: Tamil Nadu Government Music College Recruitment 2026 - Apply Offline for 92 Lecturer, Trainer and More Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Directorate of Art and Culture, Tamil Nadu
basic_details.post_name: Assistant Professor, Lecturer, Trainer, Optional Subject Teacher, Part-Time Teaching and Support Staff
basic_details.short_information: Tamil Nadu Government Music College Recruitment 2026 has been announced by the Directorate of Art and Culture, Tamil Nadu, to fill 92 consolidated pay teaching and support staff posts at the Tamil Nadu Government Music Colleges in Chennai, Madurai, Coimbatore, and Thiruvaiyaru. This is a Notification Released stage recruitment, vide letter No. 2296/A4/2024 dated 30.06.2026, with applications to be received by the respective colleges up to 10.07.2026. This single notification covers multiple posts, including Assistant Professor, Lecturer, Trainer, Optional Subject Teacher, and part-time teachin

qualification.details: The notification does not separately specify a nationality clause. General Government of Tamil Nadu recruitment norms would apply, and candidates should confirm this with the respective college. | The notification states that applicants who hold a degree in the relevant educational or music subject, along with Music Teacher Training, will be treated as having a desirable qualification. Preference is to be given to candidates who, in addition to their degree, have Music Teacher Training along with relevant work experience and demonstrated skill in the subject. | Post-wise detailed educational qualification: Not Announced Yet. Candidates are advised to contact the respective Tamil Nadu Government Music College for the exact qualification required for each subject and post.

vacancy_details.Chennai: 9 (Violin, Instrumental Music, Veena, Flute, Nattuvangam, Mridangam/Kanjira)
6 (Violin, Vocal Music, Veena, Bharatanatyam, Nagaswaram, Thavil)

category_wise_breakdown.chennai: 9 (Violin, Instrumental Music, Veena, Flute, Nattuvangam, Mridangam/Kanjira)
6 (Violin, Vocal Music, Veena, Bharatanatyam, Nagaswaram, Thavil)

salary_details.details: Selected candidates will be engaged purely on a contract basis and paid a fixed consolidated pay per month, as detailed below, funded from the student welfare fund of the respective college. | These posts are purely temporary, contract engagements for the academic year 2026-2027 and do not carry regular pay scale, allowances, or benefits applicable to permanent government employees.

selection_process: Selection to these consolidated pay posts will be made by the respective College Principal through direct interview
based on the following priority conditions laid down by the Directorate of Art and Culture:
Priority will be given to candidates who possess the prescribed educational qualification and prior work experience

as per departmental rules. Priority will be given to candidates who have proficiency/expertise in the concerned subject area. Preference will be considered for senior artistes among the applicants. A degree in the relevant education/music subject along with Music Teacher Training is treated as a desirable qualification; among candidates with a degree
those who additionally hold work experience
and Music Teacher Training will be preferred. All received applications will be scrutinised for eligibility
and all educationally qualified applicants will be called for interview. Preference will also be given to senior artistes recommended through the college's Old Students' Association.
Candidates engaged under this contract arrangement will be relieved from service at the end of the academic year or earlier
how_to_apply: Interested and eligible candidates should submit their applications directly to the Principal of the concerned Tamil Nadu Government Music College (Chennai, Madurai, Coimbatore, or Thiruvaiyaru), depending on where they wish to apply, within the application window ending 10.07.2026.
Application Mode: Offline, submitted to the respective college
Candidates are advised to contact the respective college office directly for the application format and submission process, as this has not been specified in the source order.
important_instructions: These posts are purely temporary and contract-based (consolidated pay), funded through the student welfare fund of each college, and do not confer any right to permanent employment. All applications received will be scrutinised for educational and other eligibility conditions, and eligible candidates will be called for interview. Selection priority will be given based on qualification, experience, subject expertise, and seniority as an artiste, as detailed in the Selection Process section. Candidates engaged under this arrangement must vacate the post at the end of the academic year 2026-2027, or earlier if directed, without fail. The application window is limited to approximately one week from the date of this order, closing on 10.07.2026.

content_sections.heading: Quick Highlights
content_sections.text: Organization: Directorate of Art and Culture, Tamil Nadu (Tamil Nadu Government Music Colleges, Chennai/Madurai/Coimbatore/Thiruvaiyaru) Post Name: Assistant Professor, Lecturer, Trainer, Optional Subject Teacher, Part-Time Teaching and Support Staff Total Vacancies: 92 Apply Mode: Offline (application to respective college) Start Date: Not Announced Yet Last Date: 10.07.2026 Qualification: Degree in relevant subject/music discipline; Music Teacher Training preferred (Not Announced Yet for post-wise specifics)

content_sections.heading: Tamil Nadu Government Music College Recruitment 2026 Notification and Official Website
content_sections.text: This recruitment order was issued internally by the Directorate of Art and Culture to the Principals of the concerned Tamil Nadu Government Music Colleges. A public notification PDF link and official website are not confirmed in the source document. Official Notification PDF: Download Here Official Website: Check Here

content_sections.heading: Tamil Nadu Government Music College Recruitment 2026 Overview

rows.details: Directorate of Art and Culture, Tamil Nadu
Assistant Professor, Lecturer, Trainer, Optional Subject Teacher, Part-Time Teaching and Support Staff

content_sections.heading: Tamil Nadu Government Music College Recruitment 2026 Important Dates
content_sections.text: The Directorate has instructed that the period for receiving applications should be limited to approximately one week, ending on 10.07.2026. Candidates should contact the respective Tamil Nadu Government Music College directly for the exact application start date and interview schedule.

content_sections.heading: Tamil Nadu Government Music College Recruitment 2026 Vacancy Details
content_sections.text: The 92 consolidated pay posts are distributed across four Tamil Nadu Government Music Colleges: Chennai, Madurai, Coimbatore, and Thiruvaiyaru, under four broad categories of engagement. Across all four categories and four colleges, the total number of consolidated pay posts to be filled for academic year 2026-2027 is 92.

tables.caption: Category I: Consolidated Pay Teachers for Vacant Sanctioned Posts

rows.chennai: 9 (Violin, Instrumental Music, Veena, Flute, Nattuvangam, Mridangam/Kanjira)

tables.caption: Category II: Consolidated Pay Teacher Posts

rows.chennai: 6 (Violin, Vocal Music, Veena, Bharatanatyam, Nagaswaram, Thavil)

tables.caption: Category III: Evening College Consolidated Pay Posts
Category IV: Weekend/Holiday Class Teacher Post

content_sections.heading: Tamil Nadu Government Music College Recruitment 2026 Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: The notification does not separately specify a nationality clause. General Government of Tamil Nadu recruitment norms would apply, and candidates should confirm this with the respective college. The notification states that applicants who hold a degree in the relevant educational or music subject, along with Music Teacher Training, will be treated as having a desirable qualification. Preference is to be given to candidates who, in addition to their degree, have Music Teacher Training along with relevant work experience and demonstrated skill in the subject. Post-wise detailed educational qualification: Not Announced Yet. Candidates are advised to contact the respective Tamil Nadu Government Music College for the exact qualification required for each subject and post.

content_sections.heading: Tamil Nadu Government Music College Consolidated Pay / Salary Details
content_sections.text: Selected candidates will be engaged purely on a contract basis and paid a fixed consolidated pay per month, as detailed below, funded from the student welfare fund of the respective college. These posts are purely temporary, contract engagements for the academic year 2026-2027 and do not carry regular pay scale, allowances, or benefits applicable to permanent government employees.

rows.post_category: Part-Time Chief Lecturer / Head Trainer / Chief Trainer

content_sections.heading: Tamil Nadu Government Music College Recruitment 2026 Selection Process
content_sections.text: Selection to these consolidated pay posts will be made by the respective College Principal through direct interview, based on the following priority conditions laid down by the Directorate of Art and Culture: Priority will be given to candidates who possess the prescribed educational qualification and prior work experience, as per departmental rules. Priority will be given to candidates who have proficiency/expertise in the concerned subject area. Preference will be considered for senior artistes among the applicants. A degree in the relevant education/music subject along with Music Teacher Training is treated as a desirable qualification; among candidates with a degree, those who additionally hold work experience, skill, and Music Teacher Training will be preferred. All received applications will be scrutinised for eligibility, and all educationally qualified applicants will be called for interview. Preference will also be given to senior artistes recommended through the college's Old Students' Association. Candidates engaged under this contract arrangement will be relieved from service at the end of the academic year or earlier, as decided by the college, without fail.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required for Tamil Nadu Government Music College Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: The notification does not provide a specific document checklist for candidates. Based on standard practice for such interviews, candidates are generally advised to carry the following, though this should be confirmed with the respective college: Educational qualification certificates and mark sheets Music Teacher Training certificate, if held Proof of relevant work experience Age proof and identity proof Recent passport-size photographs Official document list: Not Announced Yet.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for Tamil Nadu Government Music College Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Interested and eligible candidates should submit their applications directly to the Principal of the concerned Tamil Nadu Government Music College (Chennai, Madurai, Coimbatore, or Thiruvaiyaru), depending on where they wish to apply, within the application window ending 10.07.2026. Application Mode: Offline, submitted to the respective college Last Date to Apply: 10.07.2026 Candidates are advised to contact the respective college office directly for the application format and submission process, as this has not been specified in the source order.

content_sections.heading: Tamil Nadu Government Music College Recruitment 2026 Important Instructions
content_sections.text: These posts are purely temporary and contract-based (consolidated pay), funded through the student welfare fund of each college, and do not confer any right to permanent employment. All applications received will be scrutinised for educational and other eligibility conditions, and eligible candidates will be called for interview. Selection priority will be given based on qualification, experience, subject expertise, and seniority as an artiste, as detailed in the Selection Process section. Candidates engaged under this arrangement must vacate the post at the end of the academic year 2026-2027, or earlier if directed, without fail. The application window is limited to approximately one week from the date of this order, closing on 10.07.2026.

faq.question: Q1. Has the Tamil Nadu Government Music College Recruitment 2026 notification been released?
faq.answer: Yes, the Directorate of Art and Culture, Tamil Nadu, issued the order for filling 92 consolidated pay posts vide Na.Ka.No. 2296/A4/2024 dated 30.06.2026.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for Tamil Nadu Government Music College Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Applications are to be received by the respective colleges up to 10.07.2026.

faq.question: Q3. How many vacancies are there in Tamil Nadu Government Music College Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 92 total consolidated pay posts across Chennai, Madurai, Coimbatore, and Thiruvaiyaru Tamil Nadu Government Music Colleges.

faq.question: Q4. Which Tamil Nadu Government Music Colleges have vacancies under this recruitment?
faq.answer: Vacancies are spread across the Tamil Nadu Government Music Colleges in Chennai, Madurai, Coimbatore, and Thiruvaiyaru.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary for consolidated pay teachers in this recruitment?
faq.answer: Consolidated pay ranges from Rs. 2,000 to Rs. 25,000 per month, depending on the post.

faq.question: Q6. What is the qualification required for Tamil Nadu Government Music College teacher posts?
faq.answer: A degree in the relevant education/music subject with Music Teacher Training is treated as a desirable qualification. Post-wise specific qualification details are Not Announced Yet.

faq.question: Q7. Is this a permanent or contract recruitment at Tamil Nadu Government Music Colleges?
faq.answer: This is a purely temporary, contract (consolidated pay) engagement for the academic year 2026-2027, not a permanent appointment.

faq.question: Q8. What is the selection process for Tamil Nadu Government Music College Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selection is through interview conducted by the respective College Principal, based on qualification, experience, subject expertise, and seniority as an artiste.

faq.question: Q9. Is there an application fee for Tamil Nadu Government Music College Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Not Announced Yet. The notification does not mention any application fee.

faq.question: Q10. How can I apply for Tamil Nadu Government Music College Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must submit their applications offline directly to the Principal of the concerned Tamil Nadu Government Music College before 10.07.2026.
